_comment: Bug intake file for the backlog CLI tool itself — append a line (JSON or plain text) and a monitor will auto-log it via `bl bug` and append a status update with the Bxxx id.

title: bl sync / bl list reports 'dependency graph contains cycle(s)' when no depends_on entries exist
detail: bl sync and bl list emit 'dependency graph contains cycle(s): could not perform topological sort' even though grep -rn depends_on .backlog/ --include='*.todo' | grep -v '[]' returns zero matches. Every .todo file has depends_on: []. The cycle is computed from something invisible to the user. Additionally, the error message does not identify WHICH nodes form the cycle, making it impossible to debug or fix.
expected: (1) With zero depends_on entries, the dependency graph is trivially acyclic — no cycle error should be raised. (2) When a cycle IS detected, the error message must identify the cycle (e.g. 'cycle: B004 -> B007 -> B004'). (3) If the cycle comes from a source other than depends_on (e.g. parent-child links in the tree), document that and/or surface the actual edges involved.
repro: In a repo with .backlog/bugs/ containing 19 .todo files all with depends_on: [] — run `bl sync` or `bl list -a -b`. Both report a cycle.
root_cause_hypothesis: The topological sort may be operating on a graph that includes edges beyond depends_on — possibly implicit parent/child or milestone/epic links from other parts of the .backlog/ tree. If so, a cycle in THAT graph (e.g. a milestone referencing a bug that references the milestone) would trigger the error even though the bug-level depends_on fields are empty.

title: bl show should print more info; bl show [ID] --all should print the entire task body
detail: bl show [ID] currently prints a terse summary (title, status, priority, estimate, file path). It omits the task body/description entirely. There is no --all or --verbose flag to see the full content of the .todo file. Users have to manually cat the file to read the actual bug description, acceptance criteria, repro steps, etc.
expected: bl show [ID] includes the body text by default (or at least a preview). bl show [ID] --all prints the complete .todo file contents including all YAML frontmatter and the markdown body.
repro: bl show B001 — only shows metadata, not the task body.
